confirmed.locus: src/data/trialData.ts / ninds-trial / efficacyResults + howToReadChart + bottomLineSummary + bedsidePearl + legend (lines ~568, 576, 585, 620-627, 660, 674-678)
confirmed.issue: NINDS 42.6% vs 27.2% is labeled 'Minimal disability (mRS 0-1) at 3 months' throughout, but the published NINDS Part 2 mRS 0-1 rate was 39% vs 26%.
confirmed.rationale: NINDS NEJM 1995 (verified via WikiJournalClub reproduction of the primary Part 2 outcome table) gives four favorable-outcome proportions: Barthel 50%/38%, mRS 39%/26% (OR 1.7, 95% CI 1.1-2.6, P=0.019), Glasgow 44%/32%, NIHSS 31%/20%. None equals 42.6%/27.2%. The repo's own absoluteReduction.info (line 606) already concedes OR 1.7 (1.2-2.6, P=0.008) is the GLOBAL composite across four scales, yet the 42.6%/27.2% percentages are still labeled 'mRS 0-1'. Whatever 42.6%/27.2% represents, it is not the mRS 0-1 rate, so the 'mRS 0-1' label is wrong.
confirmed.source: NINDS rt-PA Study Group, NEJM 1995;333:1581-1587 (per-scale favorable-outcome table via WikiJournalClub + AHA reanalysis via web search); PMID 7477192
confirmed.proposedFix: Either (a) correct the mRS 0-1 rates to 39% vs 26% (NNT ~7.7, OR 1.7 95% CI 1.1-2.6, P=0.019) in efficacyResults/chart/legend/bottomLine/bedsidePearl, keeping NNT internally consistent; or (b) relabel the 42.6%/27.2% figures as the 'global favorable-outcome composite' and cite all four scales, not 'mRS 0-1'. Do NOT mix: the current state pairs the composite-labeled OR with an mRS-labeled percentage. Resolve the provenance of 42.6%/27.2% before writing (it matches no single published scale proportion).

confirmed.issue: SWIFT PRIME ordinalStats records commonOR 2.75 (95% CI 1.53-4.95); the published primary mRS-shift common OR was 2.63 (95% CI 1.57-4.40).
confirmed.rationale: Saver et al., NEJM 2015;372:2285-2295 (PMID 25882376, DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1415061) is the primary source; the NEJM full text (verified via web search of the NEJM PDF) reports the mRS-shift common OR 2.63, 95% CI 1.57-4.40, P<0.001. The PubMed abstract corroborates the shift result (P<0.001) and functional independence 60% vs 35%. Repo value 2.75 (1.53-4.95) matches neither the point estimate nor either CI bound. The error is duplicated in legend.keyStat 'cOR 2.75 (1.53-4.95)'.
confirmed.source: Saver et al., NEJM 2015;372:2285-2295; PMID 25882376; DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1415061 (NEJM full text via web search; PubMed abstract via MCP)
confirmed.proposedFix: Set ordinalStats to commonOR 2.63, ciLow 1.57, ciHigh 4.40 (pValue stays <0.001), and update legend.keyStat to 'cOR 2.63 (1.57-4.40)'.

confirmed.issue: stats.effectSize shows '+31% mRS 0-2 Benefit' (a secondary 90-day outcome) while stats.primaryEndpoint is labeled 'Reperfusion / 24-Hour Coprimary Endpoint', juxtaposing a secondary effect size against the co-primary label.
confirmed.rationale: EXTEND-IA had two binary co-primaries: 24h reperfusion (median 100% vs 37%) and early neuro improvement at day 3 (80% vs 37%, P<0.001) - confirmed via Campbell NEJM 2015 abstract/summaries. The 71% vs 40% mRS 0-2 (+31 pp) is the SECONDARY 90-day outcome; the repo itself labels it 'secondary' in howToReadChart[0] (line 2226) and howToInterpret (line 2239). Placing '+31% mRS 0-2' in the effectSize slot beneath a 'Coprimary Endpoint' primaryEndpoint label conflates a secondary effect with the co-primary. This is a display/labeling inconsistency, not a wrong number; the value 31 pp is itself correct.
confirmed.source: Campbell et al., NEJM 2015;372:1009-1018; PMID 25671797; DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1414792 (co-primary values confirmed via web search + WikiJournalClub/ACC summaries)
confirmed.proposedFix: Either relabel stats.effectSize to 'Secondary mRS 0-2 benefit' for transparency, or replace it with a co-primary measure (e.g., '+63% median reperfusion' or '+43 pp early neuro improvement'). Prefer explicit 'secondary' labeling so the effectSize matches the transparency already present in howToReadChart[0].

confirmed.issue: efficacyResults percentage bars display reperfusion rates (79.4% vs 84.5%, procedural surrogate) rather than the primary functional (mRS-shift) endpoint; reperfusion directionally favors the bridging/control arm.
confirmed.rationale: DIRECT-MT (Yang NEJM 2020, PMID 32374959) is a noninferiority ordinal mRS-shift trial; the primary result is adjusted common OR 1.07 (95% CI 0.81-1.40), correctly shown in stats.effectSize, howToReadChart, howToInterpret and legend.keyStat. The efficacyResults bar block, however, headlines 'Overall successful reperfusion' 79.4% vs 84.5% - a procedural surrogate, not the primary/principal functional endpoint. Functional independence (mRS 0-2) exists in the entry only inside howToReadChart[1] (62.0% vs 58.5%), not in the headline efficacyResults. Displaying reperfusion (which favors control) as the headline efficacy result can mislead. Confirmed as a display-choice discrepancy, not a wrong statistic.
confirmed.source: Yang et al., NEJM 2020;382:1981-1993; PMID 32374959; DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2001123 (noninferiority mRS-shift design; mRS 0-2 rates corroborated via web search, though sources vary 62/58.5 vs 65/57 by analysis population)
confirmed.proposedFix: Move reperfusion (79.4% vs 84.5%) into safetyProfile or a procedural-metrics field, and populate efficacyResults with the functional endpoint - either the mRS 0-2 rates (as in howToReadChart[1], with a source-consistency check on 62.0/58.5 vs the 65/57 primary-analysis figures) or a descriptive cOR 1.07 (0.81-1.40) presentation. Verify the exact mRS 0-2 numbers against the primary paper before writing, since summary sources disagree.

confirmed.issue: stats.primaryEndpoint labels 'BCVA Change at 1 Month' (the continuous primary, P=0.69) while stats.effectSize shows '-2.9% No Benefit' derived from the secondary dichotomized outcome (57.1% vs 60.0%); the two stats fields reference different outcomes without labeling.
confirmed.rationale: EAGLE (Schumacher, Ophthalmology 2010, DOI 10.1016/j.ophtha.2010.03.061) had a continuous BCVA primary (no difference, P=0.69) and a secondary dichotomized visual-improvement outcome (57.1% LIF vs 60.0% CST) - both stated verbatim in the repo's own armDetails (line 1342-1343) and howToReadChart (line 1429). The '-2.9%' in stats.effectSize is exactly 57.1-60.0, i.e. the SECONDARY binary difference, sitting under a primaryEndpoint labeled 'BCVA Change'. So within the stats block the effectSize is not a BCVA-change magnitude - it is the unlabeled secondary. Confirmed as an internal field-inconsistency. Severity low: the trial is negative on both endpoints, so no clinical action is misdirected; the number itself is arithmetically correct.
confirmed.source: Schumacher M et al., Ophthalmology 2010;117:1367-1375; DOI 10.1016/j.ophtha.2010.03.061 (endpoint structure confirmed from in-repo armDetails/howToReadChart which cite the primary paper; both endpoints negative)
confirmed.proposedFix: Label the efficacyResults/effectSize explicitly as the secondary dichotomized outcome (e.g., effectSize label 'Secondary: ≥15-letter gain, no benefit'), or replace effectSize with a primary-endpoint descriptor (mean BCVA/logMAR change difference, NS, P=0.69). Cosmetic/transparency fix - the negative conclusion is unaffected.

confirmed.issue: EXTEND p=0.04 shown as 'Statistically Sig.' in headline stats without the qualifier that significance is adjusted-analysis-only (unadjusted RR 1.2, P=0.35; prespecified logistic OR 1.88, P=0.056)
confirmed.rationale: Verified against thebottomline.org.uk summary of Ma NEJM 2019: adjusted RR 1.44 (1.01-2.06) P=0.04, unadjusted RR 1.2 (0.82-1.76) P=0.35, prespecified logistic OR 1.88 (0.99-3.59) P=0.056. Repo stats.pValue reads value '0.04' / label 'Statistically Sig.' with no adjusted-only qualifier; the nuance appears only lower in howToInterpret.cautions, not in the prominent stats block.

confirmed.issue: DEVT noninferiority decision described with two-sided 95% CI lower bound -2.9%; the published NI test uses a one-sided 97.5% CI lower bound of -5.1%
confirmed.rationale: PMC7816099 (Zi, JAMA 2021) states 'difference, 7.7%, 1-sided 97.5% CI, -5.1% to infinity; P for noninferiority = .003'. Repo Q2 asserts 'The lower CI bound (-2.9 pp) cleared this margin', and keyStat is '+7.7% (-2.9 to 18.2)' — the -2.9 is a two-sided descriptive bound, not the NI-decision bound. Displaying -2.9 as the decisive bound understates how close the trial ran to the -10 pp margin and misstates the method.
confirmed.source: PMC7816099 / JAMA 2021;325:234-243 (DEVT primary results); cross-checked repo lines 2898, 2902, 2921
confirmed.proposedFix: Present the NI decision using the one-sided 97.5% CI: lower bound -5.1% (P for NI=0.003). Keep the two-sided 95% CI (-2.9 to 18.2) only as a separate descriptive estimate, clearly labeled, not as the NI-clearing bound. Update Q2 wording and legend.keyStat accordingly.

confirmed.issue: DEVT safety figures sICH 4.3% vs 3.4% and mortality 14.7% vs 18.8% do not match published values
confirmed.rationale: PMC7816099 (Zi, JAMA 2021) reports sICH 6.1% vs 6.8% (difference -0.8%, 95% CI -7.1 to 5.6) and 90-day mortality 17.2% vs 17.8% (difference -0.5%, 95% CI -10.3 to 9.2). The repo's 4.3/3.4/14.7/18.8 figures do not correspond to the published primary results; mortality direction is even inverted (repo shows control higher).
confirmed.source: PMC7816099 / JAMA 2021;325:234-243, Table 2 (DEVT safety outcomes); cross-checked repo line 2906
confirmed.proposedFix: Replace Q3 safety numbers with published values: sICH 6.1% (direct EVT) vs 6.8% (alteplase+EVT); 90-day mortality 17.2% vs 17.8%. Verify no other DEVT surface (safetyProfile, pearls) carries the erroneous figures.

confirmed.issue: ordinalStats.direction labeled 'positive' for a null trial (OR 1.08, CI crosses 1.0, P=0.51)

confirmed.issue: Schema-illegal pairing estimation-strategy + futility-stopped; 'futility-stopped' is also factually inaccurate (boundary not crossed, trial not stopped early)
confirmed.rationale: Schema violation verified directly in-repo: trialData.ts line 355 states 'estimation-strategy → leave both null; document in applicability prose instead', and futility-stopped is not a legal primaryResult for estimation-strategy. Factual point also verified: BEST-II (Mistry JAMA 2023, PMC10481231) is a Bayesian phase-2 futility/estimation design that enrolled its full planned 120 patients (40/arm) — it was NOT stopped early, and the futility test (P=0.93) did NOT cross the futility boundary. The schema defines futility-stopped as 'pre-specified futility boundary crossed or data clearly futile,' which is false here. Finder's diagnosis is correct; its proposed reclassification to dose-finding-safety is debatable since the design is Bayesian-estimation, not a classical dose-finding-safety comparison.
confirmed.source: src/data/trialData.ts lines 338-339/355/4527-4528; Mistry et al. JAMA 2023 BEST-II (PMC10481231) via web search — phase-2 Bayesian futility design, full 120-patient enrollment, futility boundary not crossed
confirmed.proposedFix: Per schema, retain primaryDesign 'estimation-strategy' and set primaryResult to null (document the futility non-crossing + 14-25% predicted success in applicability prose). Do NOT use 'futility-stopped'. If a result label is required by the renderer, escalate to add a schema-legal option — do not adopt the finder's dose-finding-safety suggestion without architect review. Not applied.

confirmed.issue: The primary NI absolute risk difference (-1.79%) is displayed without any confidence interval anywhere in the entry, violating the ARD-requires-CI display rule for a noninferiority trial.
confirmed.source: Oldgren et al., Circulation 2022, PMID 36065821 (DOI 10.1161/CIRCULATIONAHA.122.060666), abstract via PubMed; and .claude/skills/trial-statistics/SKILL.md lines 13 and 126
confirmed.rationale: The abstract states 'absolute risk difference, -1.79% [95% CI, -5.31% to 1.74%]; =0.004.' The skill mandates for NI trials 'the observed treatment difference, and the CI for that difference' (L13) and 'ARD requires a CI. Never show ARD without a CI' (L126). The entry shows RD -1.79% (effectSize and legend.keyStat) with no CI; howToInterpret only says the upper bound 'did not exceed the margin' without quoting it. Finder's claimed CI is source-accurate.
confirmed.proposedFix: Add the CI wherever the ARD appears: e.g., effectSize label 'ARD -1.79 pp (95% CI -5.31 to +1.74)', legend.keyStat 'RD -1.79% (95% CI -5.3 to +1.7)', and quote the +1.74 pp upper bound vs the 3 pp NI margin in howToInterpret.proves. Values per Oldgren et al. Circulation 2022.

confirmed.issue: ARD (8.8pp) shown in stats.effectSize without a confidence interval

Pull the ARD/risk-difference CI from Xiong NEJM 2024 primary text/supplement; if unavailable, derive from the control rate (24.2%) and rate ratio CI, and note the derivation. Do NOT display an ARD CI derived by hand without evidence-verifier sign-off.

confirmed.locus: src/data/trialData.ts — 'crest-trial' line 11147 (pearls entry 8): 'Stroke or death at 4 years (secondary)'
confirmed.issue: Secondary endpoint HR 1.50 cited without its 95% CI.
confirmed.rationale: Primary source (Brott NEJM 2010, PMC2932446) states verbatim: 'The 4-year rate of stroke or death was 6.4% in the stenting group as compared with 4.7% in the endarterectomy group (hazard ratio, 1.50; 95% CI, 1.05 to 2.15; P = 0.03).' confirmed.issue: HERMES NNT 2.6 misattributed to functional independence (binary mRS 0-2)
confirmed.rationale: The HERMES primary abstract (PubMed PMID 26898852, DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(16)00163-X) states verbatim: 'The number needed to treat... to reduce disability by at least one level on mRS for one patient was 2.6.' NNT 2.6 is the ordinal ≥1-level shift NNT, NOT the mRS 0-2 functional-independence NNT. The guide presents '46% vs 29% functional independence... (NNT 2.6)', collapsing the ordinal-shift NNT onto the binary independence outcome. Published mRS 0-2 rates 46% vs 26.5% give an independence NNT ≈ 5.1.
confirmed.source: PubMed PMID 26898852 (Goyal HERMES, Lancet 2016;387:1723-31) abstract, direct quote
confirmed.proposedFix: Separate the two metrics: state functional independence as 46% vs 26.5% (mRS 0-2) with its own NNT ~5, and present NNT 2.6 (with cOR 2.49, 95% CI 1.76-3.53) as the ordinal mRS-shift effect. Register/verify a claim ID for this pearl.

confirmed.issue: HERMES mRS 0-2 control rate stated as 29% — does not match published figure
confirmed.rationale: Published HERMES mRS 0-2 rates at 90 days are 46.0% (thrombectomy) vs 26.5% (control), ARD 19.5% (multiple concordant summaries of Goyal 2016). The guide's thrombectomy figure (46%) is correct; the control figure (29%) is wrong — should be 26.5%. NOTE: the finder's OWN proposed correction (44.6% vs 31.8%) is ALSO incorrect — 44.6% is the DEFUSE-3 thrombectomy rate (which appears one line below in the same guide), so the finder conflated sources. Correct fix is 46% vs 26.5%.
confirmed.source: Multiple concordant summaries of Goyal HERMES 2016 (46.0% vs 26.5%, ARD 19.5%); PubMed PMID 26898852 abstract confirms cOR 2.49 primary shift
confirmed.proposedFix: Change control rate from 29% to 26.5% (thrombectomy 46% is already correct). Do NOT apply the finder's 44.6%/31.8% — those are wrong. Pair with the Finding #4 fix and register a claim ID + HERMES citation.

confirmed.issue: WAKE-UP 'favorable outcomes' (53.3% vs 41.8%) omits the mRS 0-1 threshold, allowing readers to assume the more common mRS 0-2 standard and mis-compare with other stroke trials.
confirmed.source: WAKE-UP primary source (NEJM 2018, NEJMoa1804355) via Wiki Journal Club / SAFE-stroke summary; repo file lines 523,530.

The repo numbers are exactly correct but the text labels them only 'favorable outcomes' with no mRS threshold; most stroke trials report mRS 0-2, so the omission is genuinely ambiguous. Numbers right, label incomplete -> low, not medium.

confirmed.issue: SPARCL NNT stated as 53 over 4.9 years; the standard published SPARCL figure is NNT 45 (Kaplan-Meier 2.2% ARR at 5 years). Repo also self-contradicts (strokeClinicalPearls.ts:848 says NNT=46).

Repo lines: PostStrokeLipidManagement.tsx:819, trialListData.cardmeta.generated.ts:764/1306/1314, strokeClinicalPearls.ts:848.
confirmed.rationale: Published SPARCL NNT is 45 from the KM 2.2% ARR at 5y. The '53' is derivable only from the crude event-rate gap (13.1-11.2=1.9% -> ~52.6) but is not the reported figure; the same repo's pearls entry uses NNT=46, so NeuroWiki contradicts itself across surfaces. HR 1.66 (1.08-2.55) and the 16% RRR are correctly stated and verified.
confirmed.proposedFix: Standardize on the published KM figure: 'NNT ~45 over ~5 years (2.2% ARR)'. Reconcile all three surfaces (PostStrokeLipidManagement, cardmeta, strokeClinicalPearls) to one value; register/update the SPARCL citation quoted_text. Because it touches multiple clinical data surfaces and reconciles a numeric claim across files, treat as D-clinical if the claim tag/citation registry is edited; C-clinical if copy-only.

confirmed.issue: CT-before-LP threshold uses 'GCS <10' (ESCMID 2016). IDSA 2004 (dominant US standard) indicates CT for any altered level of consciousness (effectively GCS <15), so GCS 10-14 patients with raised ICP could get an unscanned LP.
confirmed.source: IDSA 2004 Practice Guidelines for Bacterial Meningitis (Tunkel, CID 2004;39:1267) — indications include 'abnormal level of consciousness' / inability to answer two consecutive questions or follow two commands, immunocompromise, seizure, papilledema, focal deficit. Repo lines 62,64.
confirmed.rationale: IDSA lists any abnormal level of consciousness (not a GCS <10 cutoff) as a pre-LP CT indication; 'GCS <10' is the ESCMID 2016 threshold. For a US-facing tool this under-triages GCS 10-14 patients, a real herniation-risk safety gap. This is a clinical-logic threshold change, so Class E, not a copy tweak.
confirmed.proposedFix: Change threshold to reflect IDSA (e.g., 'depressed/altered level of consciousness (GCS <15)') OR explicitly cite ESCMID and add a note that IDSA uses altered mental status. Register the meningitis citation (IDSA 2004 or ESCMID 2016) in the citation registry. Class E: clinical logic + guideline currency + clinical-reviewer gate.

confirmed.issue: Hemicraniectomy stated as '<60 y' with midline shift as trigger. Guideline (and repo's OWN 2026 data) uses '≤60 years' with 'malignant cerebral edema / neurological deterioration' as trigger, plus a separate >60y recommendation the guide omits.

AcuteStrokeMgmt.tsx:93 says 'if <60 y' and detail at :91 attaches 'midline shift' as trigger.
confirmed.rationale: The guide contradicts NeuroWiki's own structured guideline data: '<60' excludes exactly-age-60 patients whom the COR 1 rec at line 800 includes ('<=60'). It also omits the >60y (COR 2a) option and frames the trigger as imaging midline shift rather than the guideline's malignant edema/neurological deterioration. Off-by-one plus missing branch = clinical logic, Class E. Severity medium because impact is limited to the age-60 boundary and framing.
confirmed.proposedFix: Change '<60 y' to '<=60 y'; reframe trigger to 'malignant cerebral edema with neurological deterioration despite medical therapy'; add a brief '>60y: individualized' note. Align with aha2026StrokeGuideline.ts brainSwelling array. Class E: threshold + logic branch; needs medical-scientist + clinical-reviewer.

confirmed.issue: SBP <140 within 1h graded 'Class I, Level A'. The 2022 AHA/ASA ICH guideline grades acute SBP lowering to 140 (mild-moderate ICH, SBP 150-220) as COR 2b, LOE B-R; both cited trials (INTERACT-2 binary p=0.06; ATACH-2 negative) missed their primary endpoints.
confirmed.source: 2022 AHA/ASA Spontaneous ICH Guideline (Greenberg, Stroke 2022) via multiple secondary summaries: SBP lowering to 140 (goal 130-150) for mild-moderate ICH SBP 150-220 is COR 2b, LOE B-R; harm statement for <130. INTERACT-2 primary binary outcome non-significant (p=0.06); ATACH-2 negative primary. Repo lines 72,74.
confirmed.rationale: Displaying 'Class I, Level A' for an intervention the source rates COR 2b / LOE B-R (and that failed its primary endpoints in both cited trials) materially overstates the evidence and could drive inappropriately aggressive BP lowering — a patient-safety-relevant misgrade. Grade discrepancy verified against the guideline's own wording.
confirmed.proposedFix: Change grade to 'COR 2b, LOE B-R'; state target as SBP 140 (goal range 130-150) for mild-moderate ICH with SBP 150-220; add caution against lowering <130 (potential harm) and note INTERACT-2/ATACH-2 primary endpoints were not met (ordinal-shift benefit only). Register the 2022 ICH guideline citation with correct COR/LOE. Class E: evidence-grade/interpretation change; medical-scientist + clinical-reviewer gate.

confirmed.issue: Cerebellar ICH surgical indication stated as '>3 cm WITH neurological decline OR brainstem compression OR hydrocephalus'; the 2022 AHA/ASA ICH guideline ADDED large volume (>15 mL) as an independent indication and rates it Class 1, LOE B-NR (page says generic 'Level B').

confirmed.issue: Early-window criterion stated as 'ASPECTS ≥6', with ASPECTS 3–5 framed as SELECT2/ANGEL-ASPECT exception; 2026 guideline recommends EVT for ASPECTS 3–10 at COR 1, LOE A in the 0–6 h window.

But the repo's 2-4 week range, IVIG=PEX equivalence, and 'IVIG easier' are all substantively accurate and standard. The concern is editorial emphasis, not a factual error; the range itself is correct.
uncertain.proposedFix: Optional editorial refinement: add a soft qualifier, e.g. 'Start within 2 weeks (benefit possibly up to 4 weeks per AAN 2003).' Not a required correction. If touched, register/verify against PMID 14504313.
